title: 2D Material Thermal Conductivity From Optothermal Raman and Stokes/anti-Stokes Thermometry
classification_notes: GPT error: Unterminated string starting at: line 188 column 20 (char 11868)

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies thermal conductivity in a 2D SnSe2 flake using two Raman-based thermometry approaches: traditional optothermal Raman and Stokes/anti-Stokes Raman thermometry. The authors report a much lower uncertainty for the Stokes/anti-Stokes method and obtain thermal conductivity values of about 1.3–1.4 W m-1 K-1, compared with 3.5 ± 2.2 W m-1 K-1 from the traditional optothermal Raman analysis.
measurement_types: diffuse mismatch model (for interfacial conductance estimation)

figures.description: Title slide identifying the work, authors, affiliations, and conference context.
figures.likely_source: presentation slide / not a data figure

figures.description: Overview slide listing the presentation structure: optothermal Raman overview, governing equations, experimental parameters, SnSe2 results, and conclusion/future work.
figures.likely_source: presentation slide / not a data figure

figures.description: Conceptual overview of the optothermal Raman technique as a non-contact method for determining thermal conductivity of 2D materials.
Early optothermal Raman application to single-layer graphene, including a schematic reference to suspended samples and the governing heat equation used in that approach.
Common optothermal Raman method for supported graphene, showing the radial heat equation with interfacial conductance and Gaussian laser heating.
Proposed governing equations for conduction, convection, radiation, and interfacial/substrate effects, including definitions of thermal resistances and radiative heat transfer.

Reduced governing equation under radial symmetry assumptions, expression for the temperature-weighted average temperature, and iterative solution criterion for extracting thermal conductivity and interfacial conductance.
Experimental parameters for the SnSe2 measurement: 488 nm laser, 60x objective, 0.7 NA, SnSe2 on Cu substrate, Gaussian beam, absorption from transmission matrix method, and boundary conditions.
Result for SnSe2 using traditional optothermal Raman: thermal conductivity reported as 3.5 ± 2.2 W m-1 K-1.
figures.data_type: result value / summary plot not shown in text

figures.description: Stokes/anti-Stokes thermometry results for SnSe2, including the intensity ratio relation, temperature extraction formula, and thermal conductivity values for n=3 and n=4.
figures.data_type: equation + result value / summary plot not shown in text
figures.likely_source: experimental Raman spectra processed through thermometry model

figures.description: Conclusion slide comparing uncertainties between traditional optothermal Raman and Stokes/anti-Stokes thermometry and stating future work directions.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper implies raw Raman measurement data were collected from SnSe2 on Cu under laser heating, including Raman spectra used for traditional optothermal Raman and Stokes/anti-Stokes intensity ratios. The text does not specify file formats, instrument output files, or whether raw spectra are deposited.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or source-data statement is present in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are not explicitly described in file-level terms. Based on the methods, the underlying measurements are Raman spectra and Stokes/anti-Stokes intensities acquired with a 488 nm laser and 60x objective on SnSe2/Cu.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted thermal conductivity values (3.5 ± 2.2 W m-1 K-1; 1.307 ± 0.440 W m-1 K-1 for n=3; 1.421 ± 0.480 W m-1 K-1 for n=4), modeled temperature profiles, and fitted/derived quantities such as absorption (56.7%) and interfacial conductance estimated via the diffuse mismatch model.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: No explicit figure-source-data package is mentioned. The slides present equations and final numerical results, but the text does not indicate separate figure data files or a source-data archive.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The document is a conference presentation rather than a full journal article. Most content is methodological and equation-based, with only summary results shown in the text. The dataset appears to be a combination of experimental Raman measurements and model-derived quantities, but the paper does not provide enough detail to distinguish any deposited raw versus processed files. The absorption value is explicitly calculated using a transmission matrix method, and interfacial conductance is described as calculated through the diffuse mismatch model.
